message:
refactor(export): deep cx threading through export_jsonl_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Second caller-migration tick in the strangler-fig phase. Follows
tick 122's reports.rs pattern: route every internal storage call
in a `_with_cx` sibling through the cx-first storage siblings.

Two prerequisite storage cx-first siblings added first:
- `StorageHandle::export_segments_with_cx(cx, query)`
- `StorageHandle::export_sessions_with_cx(cx, query)`

This brings storage cx-first coverage to 48/140 methods.

Then `export_jsonl_with_cx` is rewritten from a pure pre-flight
delegate into a fully cx-threaded export body. All 7 storage calls
that `export_jsonl` performs (one per ExportKind variant, plus
`get_step_logs` nested inside Workflows) now use their cx-first
siblings so caller cancellation propagates into storage's
pre-flight checkpoints:

- export_segments → export_segments_with_cx
- export_gaps → export_gaps_with_cx
- get_events → get_events_with_cx
- export_workflows → export_workflows_with_cx
- get_step_logs → get_step_logs_with_cx
- export_sessions → export_sessions_with_cx
- get_audit_actions → get_audit_actions_with_cx
- export_reservations → export_reservations_with_cx

Legacy `export_jsonl` preserved unchanged — continues to use the
legacy storage methods.

Existing regression test `export_jsonl_with_cx_matches_legacy` pins
byte-for-byte output parity: the deepened cx-first path must produce
identical JSONL output to the legacy path on an uncancelled cx.

Verified via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/
ft-rusticmaple-target, --features asupersync-runtime): 174/174
export tests pass, including the byte-parity test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
